title: Canonical Property Profile
description: v1 of the one canonical property-profile contract (PRD section 32.3). All connectors, rules, scenarios, reports, and UI views must exchange this contract; modules may not invent competing property schemas. Every fact value carries a REQUIRED provenance_ref that must resolve to a provenance record in this profile (PRD sections 9 and 19; fix for M0-T004 G3 defect D1). Referential integrity of EVERY provenance_ref site (fact values, zoning.mapped_features, and the v1.1 district-provenance maps) is enforced by .github/scripts/validate_contracts.py for fixtures and must be enforced by the backend for live data. CONTRACT 1.1.0 (task M1-T006, additive; M1-T005 G3 adjudication #1 + defects D2/D4): documents the three keys the accepted M1-T005 API already emits (OPTIONAL per-fact coverage_status, OPTIONAL top-level data_completeness, OPTIONAL top-level reproducibility) and adds OPTIONAL machine-resolvable district-provenance maps under zoning. Every 1.0.0 instance remains valid: no required key was added, removed, or retyped.

profile_version.description: Version of this profile instance (monotonic per property) plus the contract version used.

contract_version.description: CLOSED enum of published contract versions within the v1 major line. 1.0.0 = M0-T009 shape; 1.1.0 = M1-T006 additive extension. New versions are admitted only by an accepted contract task that appends to this enum; arbitrary versions are rejected.

bins.description: Building Identification Numbers on the lot.

borough.description: Normalized borough display name (documented official spellings; see common.schema.json for grounding). Raw connector spellings are preserved in provenance original_value.

geometry.description: GeoJSON geometry of the lot. OPEN-WITH-FLAG: the full tax-lot geometry contract (CRS handling, polygon validity, geometry versioning) is deferred to the M2 tax-lot geometry tasks. Note from research (docs/research/M0-T002-geoclient-address-resolution.md section 2.6): Geoclient x/y are NAD83 New York-Long Island state plane in feet, lat/long are derived; the EPSG mapping is UNKNOWN from official pages and must be verified before geometry import.

lot_facts.description: Lot-level facts (area, frontage, depth, corner status, etc.). Material by definition: every fact value REQUIRES a provenance_ref (PRD sections 9/19).

existing_building_facts.description: Existing-building facts (floor area, stories, use, landmark status, etc.). Every fact value REQUIRES a provenance_ref.

zoning.description: Zoning districts, overlays, special districts, and mapped features. OPEN-WITH-FLAG: district-code enums/patterns are NOT modeled in v1 because the official zoning source family has not yet been researched (M0-T002 covered address/BBL/BIN only; Geoclient examples expose no zoning district designation field - research doc section 2.6). Free strings here must carry provenance; enum grounding is added additively when the M2 zoning-source research lands.

district_provenance.description: CONTRACT 1.1.0, OPTIONAL (M1-T005 G3 defect D4): machine-resolvable provenance for zoning.districts values. Each key MUST be a value present in the sibling districts array; each mapped provenance_ref MUST resolve to a provenance_id in this profile's provenance array. Both rules are enforced by validate_contracts.py for fixtures and by the backend for live data. The plain-string districts array is unchanged and remains the canonical value list; this map only annotates it, so 1.0.0 instances (no map) stay valid.

commercial_overlay_provenance.description: CONTRACT 1.1.0, OPTIONAL (G3 D4): same contract as district_provenance, keyed by values of the sibling commercial_overlays array.

special_district_provenance.description: CONTRACT 1.1.0, OPTIONAL (G3 D4): same contract as district_provenance, keyed by values of the sibling special_districts array.

project_intent.description: User-selected development objectives for the analysis. Objective identifiers are finalized with the scenario-engine contracts (M5); PRD section 6 is the source list.

provenance.description: Source provenance records for every fact used (PRD section 9). Each record follows the canonical source_fact contract. Every fact_value.provenance_ref in this profile must equal some provenance_id in this array.

missing_inputs.description: Inputs the platform could not obtain, with criticality (PRD section 12 data-completeness).

conflicts.description: Cross-source conflicts detected for facts on this property. Conflicts must remain visible in results and reports (PRD sections 2/9).

resolution.description: PLATFORM-DEFINED enum (not a government-source value): grounded in PRD section 5 (user confirms/corrects), section 9 (user confirmed/overrode), and section 8 (official source priority order). 'source_priority' means the platform preferred the higher-priority official source and the conflict remains visible.

user_confirmations.description: User confirmations and overrides of official facts (PRD sections 5/9).

data_completeness.description: CONTRACT 1.1.0, OPTIONAL (M1-T005 G3 defect D2): profile-level data-completeness status - exactly the 3 PRD section 12 values, referenced from coverage_status.schema.json (never duplicated inline). The accepted M1-T005 builder derives it deterministically from missing_inputs criticality (any critical missing input -> missing_critical; any missing input -> missing_noncritical; else complete).

reproducibility.description: CONTRACT 1.1.0, OPTIONAL (M1-T005 G3 defect D2): retrieval metadata that lets a report reader reproduce this profile build (PRD section 9: retain the exact source snapshot used; PRD section 20 item 17: reproducibility identifier). Shape matches the accepted M1-T005 builder output exactly: the builder emits every subfield unconditionally, so all subfields are REQUIRED when the object is present. Single-source (PLUTO) shape; when M2 merges multiple sources this evolves additively (e.g. a per-source array ALONGSIDE this object), never by retyping these keys.

correlation_id.description: Correlation id of the request that built this profile (PRD section 25 structured logs; equals the X-Correlation-ID response header of the M1-T005 endpoint). The reproducibility identifier joining this profile to logs and job records (PRD section 20 item 17).

source_id.description: Source-registry id of the official source used (PRD sections 8.2/9), e.g. 'nyc-dcp-pluto-soda'.

dataset_id.description: Official dataset identifier within the source (PRD section 9 'dataset version or page/document version' context), e.g. SODA id '64uk-42ks'.

dataset_version.description: Dataset version used for this build, e.g. PLUTO release '26v1' (PRD section 9). REQUIRED key, explicitly null when the source publishes/exposes none at retrieval time (same visible-absence pattern as source_fact.effective_date); never an empty string.

request_url.description: Exact official request URL used, so the retrieval can be repeated against the same endpoint (PRD section 9 provenance; secrets/tokens are never part of this URL - they travel in headers).

retrieved_at.description: Timestamp of the successful source retrieval this profile was built from (PRD section 9 retrieved timestamp).

record_count.description: Number of source records the retrieval returned (PRD section 9 snapshot completeness; >1 signals an unexpected multi-record response kept visible rather than silently truncated).

drift_signals.description: Connector schema-drift signals observed during retrieval (PRD section 25 schema-drift alerts), e.g. 'non_finite_number_value:lotarea'. Facts named by drift signals carry coverage_status 'unsupported'. Empty array = no drift.

connector_notes.description: Connector notes carrying official data-dictionary semantics for present-but-unknown values (e.g. 'yearbuilt_unknown: ...'); feeds missing_inputs. Conflicts and stale data stay visible (PRD principle 4).

coverage_policy.description: Verbatim statement of the deterministic policy that produced every coverage_status in this profile (PRD section 12: AI/connector confidence never substitutes for legal-review status). Stored per profile so a historical report explains its own labels even after the policy text evolves.

provenance_ref.description: REQUIRED id of the provenance record backing this fact (PRD section 19: a material calculation can never be exported without provenance). Must resolve to a provenance_id in this profile's provenance array.

coverage_status.description: CONTRACT 1.1.0, OPTIONAL (M1-T005 G3 defect D2): PRD section 12 coverage status of this fact - exactly the 6 canonical values, referenced from coverage_status.schema.json (never duplicated inline). OPTIONAL so every 1.0.0 instance stays valid. Derived only from review status and conflict/drift state, never from connector confidence, and never 'verified' before a published-rule/reviewer pipeline exists (see reproducibility.coverage_policy).

provenance_ref_list.description: Non-empty list of provenance_ref ids; every entry must resolve to a provenance_id in this profile's provenance array (PRD sections 9/19). A list (not a single ref) so one value can be corroborated by multiple provenance records - e.g. PLUTO zonedist columns today plus the M2 DCP GIS zoning-features cross-check later - without another contract bump, and so duplicate source columns can never silently drop a record.

district_provenance_map.description: CONTRACT 1.1.0 (G3 D4): map from a zoning value string (a member of the sibling plain-string array) to the provenance records backing it. Two validator-enforced integrity rules: (1) every key is present in the sibling value array; (2) every listed provenance_ref resolves to a provenance_id in this profile's provenance array. The map may cover a subset of the array (partial linkage is legal; absent linkage means provenance is joinable only via provenance[].original_field_name, the documented 1.0.0 situation).
